edges.description: Constitutively active FGFR2 sustains downstream MAPK/ERK signaling.
edges.target: Sustained MAPK/ERK signaling in suture osteoblasts

edges.description: ERK/MAPK signaling drives the suture osteogenic program.
edges.source: Sustained MAPK/ERK signaling in suture osteoblasts

edges.description: Excess suture osteogenesis precipitates premature suture fusion.
Premature multi-suture fusion manifests clinically as craniosynostosis.
Altered cranial and facial growth produces midface hypoplasia/retrusion.
Craniofacial skeletal restriction and shallow orbits manifest as ocular proptosis.
Orbital and craniofacial abnormalities can disrupt ocular alignment.
Restrictive craniosynostosis can elevate intracranial pressure.
Posterior fossa and cranial-base effects of syndromic craniosynostosis can manifest as Chiari malformation.

nodes.description: ERK1/2-driven FGFR2 signaling accelerates osteoblast differentiation and matrix mineralization within cranial suture mesenchyme, producing excess osteogenic activity across the suture.

evidence.reference_title: Fibroblast growth factor receptor 2 promotes osteogenic differentiation in mesenchymal cells via ERK1/2 and protein kinase C signaling.
evidence.snippet: Both WT and MT FGFR2 increased early and late osteoblast gene expression and matrix mineralization.

evidence.reference_title: Fibroblast growth factor receptor 2 promotes osteogenic differentiation in mesenchymal cells via ERK1/2 and protein kinase C signaling.
evidence.snippet: Using dominant-negative ERK and PKCalpha vectors, we demonstrated that WT and MT FGFR2 promoted osteoblast gene expression through ERK1/2 and PKCalpha signaling, respectively.

nodes.description: Chiari malformation is a frequent central nervous system structural manifestation in Crouzon syndrome.

evidence.snippet: FGFR2 | HGNC:3689 | Crouzon syndrome | MONDO:0007405 | AD | Definitive

nodes.description: Mutations in FGFR2, predominantly in the third immunoglobulin-like domain (Ig-III) and linker regions, cause constitutive receptor activation or altered ligand binding. This leads to premature osteogenic differentiation in cranial suture mesenchyme and suture fusion.

evidence.reference_title: Fibroblast growth factor receptor 3 (FGFR3) transmembrane mutation in Crouzon syndrome with acanthosis nigricans.
evidence.snippet: we have identified 10 different mutations in the FGFR2 extracellular immunoglobulin III (IgIII) domain in 50% (16/32) of our Crouzon syndrome patients

nodes.description: Widely spaced eyes due to craniofacial dysmorphism.
Increased intracranial pressure can accompany restrictive craniosynostosis.
Midface hypoplasia is a hallmark feature, causing the characteristic facial appearance with relative mandibular prognathism.

evidence.reference_title: Fibroblast growth factor receptor 3 (FGFR3) transmembrane mutation in Crouzon syndrome with acanthosis nigricans.
evidence.snippet: Crouzon syndrome, an autosomal dominant condition characterized by craniosynostosis, ocular proptosis and midface hypoplasia

nodes.description: Premature fusion of multiple sutures including coronal, sagittal, and lambdoid, causing variable skull shape abnormalities.

evidence.snippet: HP:0011324 | Multiple suture craniosynostosis | Very frequent (99-80%)

evidence.reference_title: Fibroblast growth factor receptor 3 (FGFR3) transmembrane mutation in Crouzon syndrome with acanthosis nigricans.
evidence.snippet: Crouzon syndrome, an autosomal dominant condition characterized by craniosynostosis, ocular proptosis and midface hypoplasia

nodes.description: Excess osteogenic activity prematurely fuses multiple cranial sutures, producing the craniosynostosis, ocular proptosis, and midface hypoplasia that define Crouzon syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Fibroblast growth factor receptor 3 (FGFR3) transmembrane mutation in Crouzon syndrome with acanthosis nigricans.
evidence.snippet: Crouzon syndrome, an autosomal dominant condition characterized by craniosynostosis, ocular proptosis and midface hypoplasia

nodes.description: Shallow orbits cause significant ocular proptosis, which may lead to exposure keratopathy and vision problems.

evidence.reference_title: Fibroblast growth factor receptor 3 (FGFR3) transmembrane mutation in Crouzon syndrome with acanthosis nigricans.
evidence.snippet: Crouzon syndrome, an autosomal dominant condition characterized by craniosynostosis, ocular proptosis and midface hypoplasia

nodes.description: Ocular misalignment is a frequent eye manifestation in Crouzon syndrome.

Constitutively active FGFR2 sustains MAPK/ERK signaling in osteoblast-lineage cells of the cranial suture. This is the conserved effector branch shared across the FGFR craniosynostoses, coupling the activated receptor to the suture osteogenic program.
nodes.id: Sustained MAPK/ERK signaling in suture osteoblasts

evidence.reference_title: Fibroblast growth factor receptor 2 promotes osteogenic differentiation in mesenchymal cells via ERK1/2 and protein kinase C signaling.
evidence.snippet: WT and MT FGFR2 induced ERK1/2 but not JNK or PI3K/AKT phosphorylation.

nodes.description: Crouzon-associated FGFR2 Ig-III domain missense variant illustrating the allelic heterogeneity of non-Apert FGFR2 craniosynostosis.

evidence.snippet: Five different mutations were found including two novel (W290G, C342W) and two previously reported, recurrent mutations for Crouzon syndrome (A344A, S354C), and one new mutation for Jackson-Weiss syndrome (C342R)

nodes.description: Crouzon-associated FGFR2 missense variant in exon IIIa, a region shared by the IIIb and IIIc isoforms, associated with atypical epithelial-derived anomalies in addition to craniosynostosis.

evidence.snippet: The W290G mutation was found in exon IIIa which is common to both alternatively spliced forms of FGFR2, BEK (expressed predominantly in primordial bones) and KGFR (expressed preferentially in epithelia). Atypical Crouzon syndrome features of epithelial-derived anal and/or external ear anomalies were present in the two affected family members with the mutation. This phenotype possibly reflects the expression of both mutant BEK and KGFR.
